defining-nodes/defineNodeSetRelationship.title: Defining Node Set Relationships
defining-nodes/defineNodeSetRelationship.description: Similar to defining a Unique Relationship, defining Node Set relationships is extremely useful. For example: a user may only have one unique profile, but that profile will have anywhere from zero to unlimited posts

defining-nodes/definePropertyVector.title: Defining Property Vectors
defining-nodes/definePropertyVector.description: Arguably defining property vectors is one of the greatest draws to using Uix for AI focused implementations. By defining a property vector, your node will be automatically embedded on those properties, allowing for seamless vector search and AI searching implementations.

defining-nodes/defineUniqueIndexes.title: Defining Unique Indexes
defining-nodes/defineUniqueIndexes.description: Defining unique indexes can be extremely useful for fetching nodes. For example if you want all users to have unique email addresses or usernames, you can define that as a unique index. This makes it extremely simple to fetch nodes using nodeKeys or indexes, and simultaneously makes it impossible for users to register an account with a username that already exists.

defining-nodes/defineUniqueRelationship.title: Defining Unique Relationships
defining-nodes/defineUniqueRelationship.description: Defining unique relationships can be extremely useful for maintaining data. For example, if you want to define a Profile for your User node, but every user should only have one profile, then you can define that as such.

defining-nodes/defining-a-basic-node.title: Defining a Basic Node
defining-nodes/defining-a-basic-node.description: Defining nodes in Uix can be very simple. No methods are necessary to generate a node without vector embeddings, unique indexes, etc.

functions/createNode.title: createNode
functions/createNode.description: A function to create a node configured in your uix.config.ts file.

functions/deleteNode.title: deleteNode
functions/deleteNode.description: A function to delete a pre-existing node in your database

functions/getAllOfNodeType.title: getAllOfNodeType
functions/getAllOfNodeType.description: A function to get a all nodes of a certain node type.

functions/getChildNodeSet.title: getChildNodeSet
functions/getChildNodeSet.description: A function to get the node set of a node type from a certain parent node.

functions/getNodeByIndex.title: getNodeByIndex
functions/getNodeByIndex.description: A function to get a pre-existing node from your database via a defined unique index.

functions/getNodeByKey.title: getNodeByKey
functions/getNodeByKey.description: A function to get a pre-existing node from your database via a nodeKey

functions/getUniqueChildNode.title: getUniqueChildNode
functions/getUniqueChildNode.description: A function to get a unique child node of a certain parent node.

functions/getVectorNodeByKey.title: getVectorNodeByKey
functions/getVectorNodeByKey.description: A function to get a pre-existing vector node from your database via the parent's nodeKey

functions/updateNode.title: updateNode
functions/updateNode.description: A function to update a pre-existing node in your database and configured in your uix.config.ts file.

getting-started/configure-env-vars.title: Environment Variables
getting-started/configure-env-vars.description: Out of the box Uix is integrated with services such as Neo4j and OpenAI. Using Neo4j is required with Uix as it is the only graph lib we currently support (more coming soon), but OpenAI use is optional.

getting-started/define-a-node.title: Define a Node Type
getting-started/define-a-node.description: Nodes in a graph database are fragments of data that are connected via edges (relationships). In this section we will define our first node to be used with Uix.

getting-started/define-config.title: Define Uix Config
getting-started/define-config.description: Uix is a CLI based function generation tool, so it only makes sense that it requires some configuration to know what to generate. This configuration should live in the root of your repo under the name uix.config.ts

getting-started/install-uix.title: Install Uix
getting-started/install-uix.description: How to install and add Uix to your project.

getting-started/running-the-cli.title: Running the CLI
getting-started/running-the-cli.description: Now that all of your configuration is created and ready to go, we can run the CLI to generate our functions.

using-uix/prerequisites.title: Prerequisites
using-uix/prerequisites.description: What do I need to use Uix?
